articles.title: Elon Musk’s SpaceXAI has been bleeding staff since its merger
articles.why: More than 50 employees leaving SpaceXAI since its merger is an early warning signal that Musk's corporate consolidations carry real human costs — and that combining cultures forcibly doesn't work, even with tech's most valued talent.
articles.hook: SpaceX and xAI merged. / 50+ people walked out the door.

articles.title: What's next for LeBron, Giannis and Kawhi? We aske...
articles.why: LeBron, Giannis, and Kawhi all facing defining offseason decisions simultaneously makes this summer one of the most consequential for NBA franchise construction in years.
articles.hook: LeBron, Giannis, Kawhi. / Three franchises, one summer to decide everything.

articles.title: The familiar legends teaching the young Spurs: Why...
articles.why: The Spurs leaning on Tim Duncan, Manu Ginobili, and David Robinson to mentor a young playoff team is a genuinely compelling story about institutional culture being passed down in real time.
articles.hook: Duncan. Manu. The Admiral. / Teaching kids how to win the Spurs way.

articles.title: YouTube viewers watch 2 billion hours of Shorts on TVs each month
articles.why: Two billion hours of Shorts watched on TVs monthly inverts every assumption about short-form video — it was built for phones, but people are watching it on the biggest screen in the house, which reshapes how advertisers and creators should think about the format.
articles.hook: Shorts were made for your phone. / People are watching 2 billion hours on TVs.
